cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Carmen Rivera wrote this letter to Disney. The question is asking what the purpose of the letter was. The options are (A) order ice-creams, (B) promise to visit again, (C) express her thanks, (D) give some suggestions. First, I need to read through the letter to understand the main point. The letter starts with Carmen talking about an enjoyable holiday at Magic Kingdom with her family. There's a story about their daughter Gloria and an ice-cream incident on Space Mountain. A staff member named Murphy helped by holding the ice-cream and then replacing it with a new one so Gloria didn't realize the original had melted. At the end of the letter, Carmen says, "We know that ___ tried hard to make our visit special. Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" So the main message here is gratitude towards Disney and the staff for their effort. The last sentence clearly shows that Carmen is thanking them.
